expectations.text: Plan contains all 12 mandatory ExecPlan sections
expectations.evidence: All sections present: Purpose, Progress, Surprises & Discoveries, Decision Log (7 entries), Outcomes & Retrospective, Context and Orientation, Plan of Work, Concrete Steps (30 numbered steps), Validation and Acceptance (11 curl-based checks), Idempotence and Recovery, Artifacts and Notes, Interfaces and Dependencies
Progress section organized by 5 milestones with ~25 checkbox items total using '- [ ]' format

expectations.text: Plan is self-contained with no external references
expectations.evidence: All context embedded. Explains Prisma, JWT, RS256, Express middleware, SendGrid in detail. No references to external docs.

expectations.text: Plan uses concrete repository-relative file paths
expectations.evidence: Comprehensive: prisma/schema.prisma, src/config/env.ts, src/middleware/auth.ts, src/middleware/rbac.ts, src/routes/auth.ts, src/services/auth.service.ts, etc.

expectations.text: Plan includes exact runnable shell commands
expectations.evidence: npm install commands with specific packages, npx prisma generate, npx prisma migrate dev, npm test, curl requests with full headers and JSON bodies

expectations.text: Validation is phrased as observable behavior
expectations.evidence: 11 acceptance checks with exact curl commands and expected HTTP status codes and JSON responses (register returns 201, login returns 200 with tokens, member gets 403 on admin endpoint, etc.)

expectations.text: Technical terms are defined in plain language
expectations.evidence: Defines: Prisma/ORM, JWT, RS256 vs symmetric signing, SHA-256 hashing, bcrypt, Express middleware, SendGrid — each with explanations of what they do and how they manifest in this project
